title: NOC 32120 – Medical Laboratory Technologists | GTR Immigration
description: Medical Laboratory Technologists under NOC 32120 are essential diagnostic professionals supporting physicians and healthcare teams across Canada. With increasing demand for laboratory testing, disease monitoring, and advanced diagnostics, this TEER 2 occupation continues to experience strong and stable employment prospects nationwide. For internationally qualified professionals, NOC 32120 provides a practical pathway toward Canada PR through Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3212 – Medical technologists and techniciansNOC Code32120

duties: Medical Laboratory Technologists perform some or all of the following duties:
Set up, operate, and maintain laboratory equipment
Conduct and interpret chemical analyses of blood, urine, and other body fluids
Examine blood cells and tissues for physiological and pathological conditions
Prepare tissue sections for microscopic examination
Perform and interpret blood group and compatibility testing
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in medical laboratory technology
Supervised practical training may be required
Registration with a provincial regulatory body is required in:OntarioAlbertaBritish ColumbiaManitobaNova ScotiaNew BrunswickNewfoundland and LabradorQuebecSaskatchewan
Certification by the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science is usually required by employers
Some employers may require a university science degree for specialized roles.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $48,200 to $86,100, depending on province and specialization.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32120 is strong and positive, particularly due to:
Professionals with advanced specialization in cytology, histotechnology, or molecular diagnostics may experience stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Medical Laboratory Technologists are employed in:
This positive gap reflects ongoing national demand, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Manitoba, and Nova Scotia.
